is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study cultured Plasmodium falciparum parasites from Ugandan children with malaria.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Examined dhfr C59R (position 59), dhps K540E (position 540), and pfmdr1 N86Y (position 86) — all curated target codons.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Frequencies are reported only for cultures that were initially mixed and became monoclonal during culture (e.g. 'Of 19 cultures that were mixed at pfmdr1 86 on day 0, 5 were subsequently monoclonal on day 8, all with the 86Y genotype'). These are culture-selection dynamics, not clinical population allele frequencies with clear numerator/denominator per locus for field prevalence.

sub_country_location.evidence: Samples from Mulago III Parish of Kampala, Uganda — a sub-national location.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Samples collected between October 2005 and May 2006 (<1 year).

african_field_samples.evidence: Human clinical isolates from children with acute malaria in Kampala, Uganda.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples collected from a cohort of children enrolled in a longitudinal drug efficacy comparison [4] (Dorsey et al. JAMA 2007), so parasites overlap with a larger published trial cohort.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary data referenced for extractable per-locus field frequencies.

assessment.summary: Study of P. falciparum complexity of infection during short-term culture of clinical isolates from Kampala, Uganda (Oct 2005-May 2006). It examined dhfr C59R, dhps K540E, and pfmdr1 N86Y but only in the context of allele selection during 9 days of culture among initially-mixed cultures, not as clinical population prevalence frequencies. It does not report extractable field allele frequencies with clear numerator/denominator per locus for the target markers.
exclusion_reasons: No extractable per-locus clinical population frequencies for the target markers — genotype data are reported only as culture-selection dynamics among mixed cultures, not as prevalence in the field sample.
